name: Attic
description: The high triangular space directly under a gabled roof. The floor of @primary is the ceiling of the room below; the walls taper inward each layer (one block off each side per level on the short axis) until they meet in a 1-block @primary ridge — or @glass for a skylight. Storage chests line the eaves (the low-headroom edge where you cannot stand), @primary blocks are stacked as crate-like boxes in the centre with one bookshelf, hanging lanterns dangle from the ridge for light, and a single dormer window cuts a small gable into one pitched wall to admit daylight.

required_furniture.rule: Lined along the eaves (the first interior row next to each long pitched wall) where the headroom is too low to stand; place 2 on the primary eave and up to 2 more on the opposite eave when the AABB is large.
Stacked @primary blocks in the centre of the floor as crate-shaped storage forms, optionally stacked 2 high under the apex where headroom allows.
A single bookshelf next to the boxes at the centre of the floor.
Hanging from the ridge (one block below the apex); scale to 3 lanterns spread along the long axis for large attics.
A 1-2 block @glass cluster cut into one long pitched wall roughly halfway up the slope; centred along the long axis.

placement_rules: Sloped walls following roof pitch — each layer above the floor shrinks by one block on each side of the short axis until reaching a 1-block ridge.
Ridge runs along the longer horizontal axis (ties break to x), mirroring the gabled_roof skill so an attic stacked below such a roof aligns geometrically.
Storage along the eaves: chests sit on the low-headroom edge where a standing person could not fit under the slope anyway.
Skylight or dormer window for light: the ridge course may be glazed (@glass instead of @primary) when daylight from above is preferred; otherwise a small dormer is cut into one pitched wall.
Hang lanterns from the ridge layer (hanging=true) so the light source dangles into the open volume below the apex.
Centre the bookshelf + stacked boxes under the apex where the headroom is highest.
lighting_ventilation: At least one hanging lantern under the ridge, plus daylight through the dormer window (and optionally a skylight ridge of @glass). Two opposite eaves with chests leave the long centre line as the ventilated walking aisle.

medieval.notes: Oak-plank ridge and floor, plain glass dormer, hanging iron lanterns dangling from the ridge beam.

modern.notes: Quartz sloped walls, a full @glass skylight ridge by default (set skylight=True), and redstone-lamp lighting in place of the lantern silhouette.

fantasy.notes: Dark-oak attic with a purple-stained-glass dormer; sea lanterns hang from the ridge giving a bluish wizard-tower glow.

python_skill_signature: pipeline.skills.attic.build(aabb: AABB, materials: Materials, style: str, **kwargs) -> list[Op]
